content: Mobile view expander pattern for ASP.NET Core 8: In ExpandViewLocations, iterate incoming viewLocations and for each path yield a mobile variant first (replacing .cshtml with .Mobile.cshtml) then yield the original as fallback. In PopulateValues, read mobile flag from context.ActionContext.HttpContext.Items and store it in context.Values['isMobile']. The cache key includes the isMobile value so desktop requests get one cached path set and mobile requests get another. Registration in Program.cs: builder.Services.Configure<RazorViewEngineOptions>(options => options.ViewLocationExpanders.Add(new MobileViewLocationExpander())); — added before builder.Build(). Layout selection for mobile is handled in _ViewStart.cshtml by reading HttpContext.Items['IsMobile'] and setting Layout conditionally.
